observations: /app/api/save-run accepts unauthenticated requests and trusts client-supplied orgId before inserting tenant-owned DSQL records (/home/labestiadevigia/phylo/app/api/save-run/route.ts:19-56).

/app/api/evolve, sandbox endpoints, and /app/api/seal-bundle perform expensive unauthenticated processing; input-size and cross-instance resource controls are incomplete (/home/labestiadevigia/phylo/app/api/evolve/route.ts:38-76; /home/labestiadevigia/phylo/app/api/seal-bundle/route.ts:41-75).

The Python timeout uses Promise.race without cancelling the underlying Pyodide execution, and Pyodide is shared through a process-global singleton (/home/labestiadevigia/phylo/app/api/sandbox/python/route.ts:109-145).

SQL validation blocks several dangerous constructs, but static validation is not equivalent to proving sandbox isolation or denial-of-service resistance (/home/labestiadevigia/phylo/app/api/sandbox/sql/route.ts:24-45).
API-key authentication, Stripe signature verification, parameterized SQL queries, and scoped bounty operations were reviewed as implemented defensive controls.

hypotheses.statement: Unauthenticated or client-controlled web inputs can cause tenant-integrity violations and resource-exhaustion exposure, especially through save-run persistence and long-running evolution/sandbox workloads.

deductions: If AURORA_DSQL_ENDPOINT is configured, an unauthenticated caller can submit arbitrary orgId, code, gene metadata, and fitness values to create records attributed to another organization.

A caller can repeatedly start costly evolution, JavaScript, Python, SQL, or bundle-sealing requests without a shared authenticated quota; process-local deduplication does not provide cross-instance protection.
A timed-out Python request may continue consuming Pyodide resources because Promise.race rejects without terminating the underlying computation.

evidence.finding: No authenticate call; body.orgId is used directly for evolution_jobs and genes inserts.
POST has no authentication, code/test size validation, or shared quota; maxGenerations is only upper-clamped.
Promise.race timeout does not cancel runInPyodide.
Pyodide instance is process-global and reused across requests.
LLM proxy has IP rate limiting and token caps, providing a partial compensating control.

decision: Treat H1 as a high-priority security hypothesis requiring authenticated tenant binding, bounded request sizes, shared quotas, and cancellable isolated execution.
Do not label the static patterns confirmed vulnerabilities; execute only approved, isolated security tests before confirmation.
Prioritize /api/save-run authorization and resource controls around evolve/sandbox/seal-bundle.

adi.statement: The observed unauthenticated persistence and expensive execution paths plausibly explain tenant-integrity and resource-exhaustion risk.
evidence: save-run trusts client orgId without authentication.
evolve and sandbox routes accept attacker-controlled workloads without shared authenticated quotas.

adi.statement: A controlled request should be able to demonstrate either cross-tenant record creation or measurable work continuing after a Python timeout, subject to an approved isolated test.
evidence: save-run inserts orgId from the request body.
Promise.race rejects without cancelling runInPyodide.

adi.statement: Static review only; no target code was executed and no exploit was run, so runtime exploitability and impact remain undetermined.
evidence: Target-repository execution was explicitly excluded.
Findings are based on source inspection and are not confirmed vulnerabilities.

skills.concrete_action: Formed H1 from the combined authorization and resource-control observations.
evidence: save-run lacks authentication and trusts orgId.
Expensive routes lack uniform shared controls.

skills.concrete_action: Checked tenant and financial mutations for identity binding and transactional boundaries.
evidence: save-run performs DSQL inserts using client-controlled orgId.
Bounty and billing paths use authenticated contexts or signed webhooks.

skills.concrete_action: Performed source audit without modifying the target.
evidence: No target files were changed.

skills.concrete_action: Attached each material claim to source file and line evidence.
evidence: Evidence entries identify exact route locations.

skills.concrete_action: Assessed exposed routes, authentication coverage, and operational controls.
evidence: Public routes have inconsistent authentication and quota behavior.

skills.concrete_action: Separated observations, predictions, and unexecuted conclusions.
evidence: Induction is abstained and explicitly states the execution limitation.

skills.concrete_action: Focused analysis on deterministic request validation and state transitions.
evidence: Reviewed direct orgId assignment and bounded generation calculations.

skills.concrete_action: Traced timeout and shared-runtime behavior to likely failure modes.
evidence: Promise.race does not cancel Pyodide work.

skills.concrete_action: Preserved repository state and excluded repository metadata from analysis.
evidence: No writes or destructive git operations were performed.

skills.concrete_action: Recorded runtime exploitability as undetermined because execution was prohibited.
evidence: No dynamic exploit validation was claimed.

skills.concrete_action: Treated LLM routes as attack surfaces and did not rely on model behavior for security.
evidence: /api/claude rate limits and caps were reviewed as controls.

skills.concrete_action: Looked for auth bypass, injection, sandbox escape, secrets exposure, and resource abuse.
evidence: Reviewed save-run, sandbox, billing, auth, SQL, and LLM routes.

skills.concrete_action: Reconstructed request-to-persistence and request-to-sandbox flows from source.
evidence: Mapped save-run body fields into DSQL columns and sandbox inputs into runtimes.

skills.concrete_action: Identified existing structural controls and missing identity binding.
evidence: Parameterized queries and Stripe HMAC exist; save-run lacks authenticated identity derivation.

skills.concrete_action: Used comments, schema, and route behavior to distinguish intended controls from actual enforcement.
evidence: Schema tenant fields were compared with route-supplied orgId behavior.

skills.concrete_action: Reviewed SQL construction for parameterization and persistence aggregation risks.
evidence: Auth, usage, and escrow queries use parameters; save-run materializes untrusted tenant metadata.

skills.concrete_action: Did not patch because the requested role was audit-only and target modification was prohibited. Reason recorded: REJECTED: modification was outside the authorized audit scope.
evidence: No patch was produced.

skills.concrete_action: Checked signed billing and bundle paths for authenticity boundaries.
evidence: Stripe webhook verifies HMAC; seal-bundle signs client-provided content without shown authorization.

skills.concrete_action: Reviewed validation at route boundaries for types, lengths, ranges, and identity.
evidence: Some routes validate fields, while save-run and sandbox inputs lack comprehensive bounds and identity checks.

skills.concrete_action: Inspected schema indexes and constraints relevant to idempotency and tenancy.
evidence: usage_events has a unique idempotency index; ledger_entries idempotency is non-unique.
